response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist web and full-stack engineer, but misses nearly every core requirement for this role: no native-level Japanese proficiency (Berlin-based, languages listed are English, Romanian, German), no substantial C-on-UNIX systems experience beyond a brief stint at National Instruments, no Linux kernel investigation or modification background, and no exposure to L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X. The gap between his web/product-engineering profile and this embedded networking-security position is too large to justify an interview.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with some low-level exposure (C/C++ at National Instruments, DLang low-latency services)
Solid Linux, Docker, and CI/CD infrastructure experience
Strong testing culture and software craftsmanship (TDD, test tooling authorship)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a hard requirement for the role
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)
No Linux kernel analysis or modification experience; C for UNIX is not a current core skill
